mechanism_narrative: Rising real retail sales (RSAFS) signal strong consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year correlation between RSAFS growth and forward 60-day returns is below 0.10 or t-statistic of regression slope is not significant at 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.5% indicates labor market weakness, reducing consumer income and spending, which damages corporate profits and depresses equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, the mean forward 20-day return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or positive returns occur in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: High levels of both the federal funds rate and CPI inflation produce a restrictive real rate environment that compounds downward pressure on equity valuations via elevated discount rates and reduced liquidity. The interaction of high rates and high inflation amplifies the negative effect beyond either variable alone.
failure_condition: The interaction term (FEDFUNDS * CPIAUCSL) in a linear regression of forward 20-day returns on FEDFUNDS, CPIAUCSL, and their product yields a coefficient not significantly negative at the 10% level over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in industrial production over three months signals expanding manufacturing output and broader economic momentum, which boosts investor confidence and corporate profitability, driving positive equity returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When INDPRO 3-month growth is positive, the mean forward 60-day return is not significantly greater than zero (one-sided t-test p < 0.05) or the signal fails to produce a positive Sharpe ratio over rolling 5-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1,400 thousand units signals a weakening housing market that leads to negative wealth effects and reduced construction activity, which depresses economic growth and equity prices over the short term.
failure_condition: When HOUST is below 1,400, the average forward 5-day return is not negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.10) or negative returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in nonfarm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, creating a persistently positive tailwind for equity prices over monthly horizons.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year correlation between monthly PAYEMS growth and forward 20-day returns is below 0.10 or the signal does not produce a positive average return differential between high- and low-growth periods (top vs bottom tercile) over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, monetary policy is highly restrictive, raising the discount rate for future cash flows and reducing market liquidity, which leads to immediate negative pressure on stock prices over the following week.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 5%, the mean forward 5-day return is not negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or positive returns occur in more than 40% of instances over any 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production declines while payrolls continue to grow signals an inventory overhang and economic imbalance that often precedes a recession. This conflicting macro signal leads to negative equity returns over the following quarter as markets price in an eventual correction.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO declining, PAYEMS rising) does not produce a significantly negative mean forward 60-day return compared to periods without divergence (two-sample t-test p > 0.10), or the signal's hit rate (negative returns) is below 55%.
